name: Abd al-Rahman al-Arhabi
title: Devoted Envoy
shortDescription: The trusted Kufan envoy who took the letters to Mecca, then returned to Kufa with Muslim ibn Aqeel, and was ultimately martyred in Karbala.
fullStory:
Abd al-Rahman ibn Abdullah al-Arhabi was a loyal and brave companion of Amir al-Mu'minin (AS) and a prominent Shia in Kufa. He played a very significant role in the correspondence.

Abd al-Rahman, along with Qays ibn Musahir, was tasked with taking the third batch of Kufan letters to Mecca and delivering them to Imam Hussain (AS). He completed this mission successfully.

Then, Imam Hussain (AS) sent him as one of the trusted guides and advisors along with Muslim ibn Aqeel to Kufa to assess the situation. After Muslim's martyrdom in Kufa, Abd al-Rahman managed to hide and leave the city.

Bypassing Ibn Ziyad's armies, he reached Imam Hussain's (AS) caravan and, on the day of Ashura, after a brave fight, was martyred either in the first attack or while defending the Imam during the noon prayer.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى عَبْدِالرَّحْمانِ بْنِ عَبْدِاللَّهِ الأَرْحَبِيِّ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Abd al-Rahman ibn Abdullah al-Arhabi.
